incident_reconstruction.reconstruction: The vehicle was involved in a head-on collision with another vehicle or object. The damage pattern indicates a severe frontal impact, likely at a significant speed.
reconstruction_bullets: The front bumper, bonnet, and headlights of the vehicle are severely damaged, indicating a high-energy impact.
The airbag appears to have deployed, suggesting a significant collision force.
The damage pattern is consistent with a head-on collision scenario as described in the claim.

storyboard_panels.title: Pre-Incident
storyboard_panels.description: The vehicle was being driven on the road when a wrong-side vehicle entered its lane.

storyboard_panels.title: Point of Impact
storyboard_panels.description: The wrong-side vehicle collided with the claimant's vehicle, causing severe front-end damage.

storyboard_panels.title: Immediate Aftermath
storyboard_panels.description: The vehicles came to a stop, and the driver assessed the damage.

storyboard_panels.title: Evidence Summary
storyboard_panels.description: The physical evidence, including severe front-end damage and airbag deployment, supports the claim of a head-on collision.

incident_reconstruction.summary: Most Probable: Front impact | Confidence: 85% | Story Match: Yes

decision_reasons: Fraud score of 40 exceeds the Approve threshold of less than 40, indicating a Medium risk
NEW POLICY SYNDROME — MEDIUM RISK indicator is present, suggesting potential for abuse
Multiple vehicles visible in claim images, raising the possibility of a staged collision scenario
Physics Mismatch indicator is present, which may indicate inconsistency in the incident description
reasoning_trail: Step 1: Damage Assessment indicates severe front-end damage consistent with a high-speed collision
Step 2: Fraud Intelligence reveals a Medium risk fraud score of 40 with indicators of NEW POLICY SYNDROME and possible staged collision
Step 3: Incident Reconstruction confirms the collision type as front impact with a confidence of 85%
Step 4: Context & Policy verification confirms the location and no obvious coverage exclusions
Step 5: Historical Precedents do not provide a direct match, but the fraud score and indicators suggest escalation
Step 6: Settlement Calculation is not applicable due to escalation decision
Step 7: Final Decision is to Escalate the claim for further investigation
